summary: Frontend-only regression testing of Portfolio site nav-anchor scroll fixes. All 5 desktop nav links, all 5 mobile nav links, the hero 'VIEW PORTFOLIO' button, project dialog + external ArtStation link, and copy-email sonner toast were verified working. Section ids (#about, #internship, #portfolio, #skills, #contact) align with nav hrefs and scroll-margin-top: 90px properly offsets the fixed nav so section headings land just below the nav.
critical_code_review_comments: Nav.jsx: data-testid uses raw label lowercased, producing 'nav-link-about me' with a space. Works but non-standard; consider slugifying to 'nav-link-about-me' for stricter selector conventions. Current tests still pass because it matches the request spec.
App.css: 'section { scroll-margin-top: 90px }' correctly compensates for the fixed nav (~76-82px tall). Verified target section top lands at ~90px after every nav click.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Portfolio site anchor navigation is fully working after id renames (#work->#portfolio, #direction->#internship) and scroll-margin-top:90px addition. Verified via window.scrollY and getBoundingClientRect().top on target sections. Desktop 5/5, mobile 5/5, hero-view-work all PASS. Dialog + external ArtStation link + copy-email sonner toast confirmed. No known blocking issues on portfolio UI.
rca of the issue: Original bug 'under THE PORTFOLIO fix the link and on CONTACTS' was caused by nav hrefs pointing to #portfolio and #contact while the actual section ids were #work and #contact (portfolio) and OK for contact. Fix in Projects.jsx (id='portfolio'), InternshipDirection.jsx (id='internship'), Hero.jsx (href='#portfolio'), plus 'section { scroll-margin-top: 90px }' in App.css so the fixed nav does not cover section tops. Verified working end-to-end.
